messages.text:
Here’s a simple, high‑leverage way to automate your info flow and give your agents better context without adding maintenance: three tiny RSS-based automations you can stand up in an evening.

---

# 1) Smart‑Digest Builder → Notion
**What it is:** Pull items from any RSS feed, filter by keywords/tickers/topics, enrich a bit, and append to a Notion database as your daily briefing.

**Why it’s useful:** Cuts noise, centralizes structured intel your agents can query (and you can review on mobile).

**Minimal build (pick one):**
- **n8n (free/self‑host)**  
  - Trigger: **RSS Feed Read** (interval 10–30 min)  
  - Process: **IF** (title/summary matches regex: e.g., `\b(NVIDIA|NVDA|robotics|macro|ETF|就业)\b`) → optional **HTTP** call to an LLM endpoint for 1‑sentence summary  
  - Sink: **Notion** → Create Page (props: `source`, `title`, `url`, `pubDate`, `tags`, `one_liner`)
- **Zapier (fast to ship)**  
  - Trigger: **RSS by Zapier: New Item**  
  - Filter: **Only continue if… title/summary contains** keywords  
  - Action: **Notion: Create Database Item**

**Notion DB fields (keep it simple):**
- Title (Title), URL (URL), Source (Select), PubDate (Date), Tags (Multi‑select), One‑liner (Text), Status (Select: To Read / Read / Archived)

**Keyword strategy (for you):**  
Split into lists: *AI/半导体*、*宏观/政策*、*机器人*、*股票/ETF*、*公司白名单*。Each list becomes a rule so you can pivot by topic later.

---

# 2) Trigger → Agent Starter (auto context windows)
**What it is:** New RSS items that pass your filters automatically open a “work item” for an agent (e.g., run a Codex workflow, create a task card, or drop a prompt into a queue).

**Why it’s useful:** You never “forget to follow up.” Items meeting thresholds spawn analysis automatically (summary, risk flags, trading notes, policy watch, etc.).

**Minimal build:**
- **n8n**  
  - Trigger: **RSS** → **IF** (e.g., has “earnings”, “监管”, “招标”, or matches a ticker watchlist)  
  - Action A: **HTTP Request** to your Agent endpoint (payload: `title`, `url`, `extracted text`, `instructions`)  
  - Action B: **Slack/Email** FYI with the agent’s first take
- **Zapier**  
  - Trigger: **RSS**  
  - Action: **Webhooks by Zapier (POST)** to your agent router  
  - Optional: **Gmail / Slack** notification with the agent’s outline

**Suggested agent prompt (drop in payload):**
```
You are “Intel Analyst (Rapid)”. Given the article, produce:
1) 8-bullet executive brief (max 120 chars each)
2) 2 near-term implications (T+7d / T+30d)
3) If investable: key drivers, red flags, data to watch
4) One question to investigate next
Return JSON with fields: bullets[], near_term[], drivers[], red_flags[], next_q.
```

---

# 3) Backup Archiver (cheap, durable)
**What it is:** Store all RSS items (raw + cleaned) into a low-cost object store for long‑term, queryable history.

**Why it’s useful:** Full corpus for RAG/analytics later; you’re not locked into any tool.

**Minimal build (choose one):**
- **Cloudflare R2 (cheap & simple)**  
  - n8n: **RSS** → **Function** (normalize JSON) → **HTTP** (PUT to R2 presigned URL)  
- **S3‑compatible** (AWS S3 / MinIO): same flow  
- **GitHub Pages/Repo (no infra):**  
  - **GitHub Actions** on schedule runs a tiny script: fetch feeds → write `YYYY/MM/DD/feed.jsonl`

**Recommended archive schema (JSONL):**
```
{
 "id": "<guid-or-url>",
 "fetched_at": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T11:00:00Z",
 "pub_date": "...",
 "source": "FT / 科创板日报 / ...",
 "title": "...",
 "url": "...",
 "summary": "...",
 "content_text": "...",
 "tags": ["ai","robotics","ETF"],
 "digest_sha256": "..."
}
```

---

## Glue pieces you’ll reuse

- **Regex filter examples**  
  - Companies/tickers: `\b(NVDA|TSLA|ASML|OpenAI|SpaceX|中芯|宁德)\b`  
  - Themes: `\b(半导体|机器人|算力|招标|产能|监管|补贴|IPO|复苏|失业|通胀|ETF)\b`
- **Dedup key:** `sha256(title + url)`  
- **Tagger:** simple keyword → tag map; keep in a JSON file so you can update without touching the workflow
- **Push channels:** Slack (fast review), Gmail API (daily rollup), Notion (system of record)
- **Rate & cost control:** poll every 15–30 min; only call LLM on items that pass filters; cap per run (e.g., 10)

---

## Quick start templates (n8n)

**A. Smart‑Digest → Notion (4 nodes)**
1) RSS Feed → 2) IF (regex) → 3) HTTP (LLM one‑liner, optional) → 4) Notion: Create Page

**B. Trigger→Agent (5 nodes)**
1) RSS → 2) IF (earnings/监管/ticker) → 3) HTTP (fetch article text via readability API) → 4) HTTP (POST to agent) → 5) Slack

**C. Archiver (3 nodes)**
1) RSS → 2) Function (normalize + sha256) → 3) HTTP PUT (R2/S3 presigned URL)

---

## Ops & hygiene
- **Config as data:** keywords, tickers, tag map, feed list in Notion or a JSON file your flows read at runtime.  
- **Observability:** add a Slack “run summary” (items seen / filtered / archived / errors).  
- **Backoff:** if a feed errors >3x, pause it for 2h.  
- **Fail‑safe:** if Notion API fails, still send to Archiver so you never lose items.  
- **Cold‑start:** seed with 200–500 recent items to train your filters and test dashboards.

---

## Where this compounds for you
- Your **Notion intel DB** becomes the single source your agents query for *grounded* summaries.  
- The **Agent Starter** ensures high‑value news automatically becomes a task with a first draft.  
- The **Archive** gives you historical recall for RAG (“what changed since last quarter?”) and quant screens later.

If you want, I can hand you:
- an n8n export (three ready‑to‑import workflows),
- a minimal Notion DB schema,
- a JSON tag map starter (AI/半导体/机器人/宏观/ETF/政策),
- and a GitHub Actions script for archive seeding.
